meanings.usage: 常用insure sth against...结构，表示为某物投保某种险
指投保人定期支付给保险公司的费用
作名词时指索赔行为；作动词时常用make a claim结构
指从灾难或水中救出财物
通常指船或类似容器的翻转
一种用电力或人力驱动的起重设备
作名词指固定用的工具；作动词指用夹子固定
指处于不稳定或高高在上的位置
指因不平衡而倒下或翻转
强调规模或程度非常大
sink的过去分词作形容词，描述沉入水中的物体
指户外的庆祝活动，通常有各种游戏和义卖

phrases.usage: 愿意做某事，表示乐意或准备做某事

examples.en: Insurance companies are normally willing to insure anything.
examples.zh: 保险公司通常愿意为任何东西保险。

phrases.usage: 如果；万一；倘若，用于引出假设的灾难情况

examples.en: It would be possible to insure yourself in the event of bad weather.
examples.zh: 如果遇到坏天气，你也可以为自己投保。

phrases.usage: 提出索赔，向保险公司要求赔偿

examples.en: A shipping company has made a claim for the cost of salvaging.
examples.zh: 一家航运公司提出了打捞费用的索赔。

examples.en: A local authority to recover the cost of salvaging.
examples.zh: 地方当局为收回打捞费用。

examples.en: The teenagers looked on while three men dived into the water.
examples.zh: 当三个人反复潜水时，青少年们在一旁观看。

examples.en: A powerful winch was put into operation.
examples.zh: 一台大功率绞车被启动运转。

examples.en: It set up a huge wave in the canal.
examples.zh: 它在运河里掀起了一股巨浪。

examples.en: It was almost impossible to attach hawsers to the rim.
examples.zh: 几乎不可能把缆绳系在边缘上。

examples.en: By working at tremendous speed, the men got the dish on to dry land.
examples.zh: 由于以极快的速度工作，这些人把盘子弄到了陆地上。

examples.en: The men were now obliged to try once more.
examples.zh: 这些人现在不得不再次尝试。

grammar.title: 被动语态 (Passive Voice)
grammar.definition: 当句子的主语是动作的承受者时，使用被动语态。被动语态由be动词+过去分词构成。
grammar.structure: 主语 + be + 过去分词 (+ by + 动作执行者)
grammar.usage: 当不知道动作执行者、强调动作承受者或上下文中不需要提及执行者时使用被动语态。

examples.en: The pie dish was insured for the trip.
examples.zh: 这个馅饼盘被投保了旅途险。

examples.en: The dish was raised to the surface and was drawn towards the canal bank.
examples.zh: 盘子被升到水面并被拖向运河岸边。

grammar.title: It is + not uncommon / not unusual to... (it作形式主语)
grammar.definition: it作形式主语代替不定式短语或从句，真正的主语是后面的不定式to结构。这种结构用于表达某种情况并不罕见。
grammar.structure: It is + not uncommon / not unusual + to + 动词原形
grammar.usage: 用来表达某事经常发生或并不稀奇，语气较委婉。

examples.en: It is not uncommon to hear that a shipping company has made a claim.
examples.zh: 听到航运公司提出索赔是很常见的。

examples.en: It is not unusual to see tourists here in summer.
examples.zh: 夏天这里有游客并不稀奇。

grammar.title: more than + n./adj. (不仅仅；超出)
grammar.definition: more than可以修饰名词、形容词或动词，表示程度'超出'或'不仅仅'的含义。
grammar.structure: 主语 + 动词 + more than + 名词/形容词/动词
grammar.usage: 用于强调数量、程度或性质超出预期或某个界限。

examples.en: Dancing proved to be more than the dish could bear.
examples.zh: 跳舞被证明是这只盘子承受不了的。

examples.en: This is more than I can afford.
examples.zh: 这超出了我的能力范围。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + be + 过去分词 + for + 目的

original.en: The pie committee decided that the best way to transport the dish would be by canal so they insured it for the trip.
original.zh: 馅饼委员会决定运输这只盘子的最好方式是通过运河，所以他们为旅途投了保。

imitations.en: The company insured the equipment for the experiment.
imitations.zh: 公司为实验设备投了保。

imitations.en: He insured his car for the journey.
imitations.zh: 他为旅途中的汽车投保了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: It is + adj. + to + 动词原形

original.en: It is not uncommon to hear that a shipping company has made a claim.
original.zh: 听到航运公司提出索赔是很常见的。

imitations.en: It is not unusual to see children playing in the park.
imitations.zh: 看到孩子们在公园里玩耍并不稀奇。

imitations.en: It is essential to understand the importance of insurance.
imitations.zh: 理解保险的重要性是必要的。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + have/had little difficulty in + doing sth

original.en: They had little difficulty in finding it.
original.zh: 他们找到它没什么困难。

imitations.en: I had little difficulty in solving this problem.
imitations.zh: 我解决这个难题没费什么力气。

imitations.en: She had little difficulty in passing the exam.
imitations.zh: 她通过考试没费什么力气。

sentencePatterns.pattern: be + adj. + enough + to + 动词原形

original.en: The dish rose to the surface and was gently drawn towards the canal bank.
original.zh: 盘子升到水面并被轻轻地拖向运河岸边。

imitations.en: The bridge was strong enough to support the weight.
imitations.zh: 这座桥足够坚固，能承受这个重量。

imitations.en: He was tall enough to reach the shelf.
imitations.zh: 他足够高，能够到那个架子。
